title: Administration of Estates (Amendment) Ordinance 2022
text:
17.В В Amendment of Law Revision and
Publication Ordinance 2017

Sections
18 and 19 amend Schedule 1 of the Law
Revision and Publication Ordinance 2017.

18.В В Part 1 of Schedule 1 of Law Revision and
Publication Ordinance amended (primary legislation)

In Part
1 of Schedule 1 вЂ”

В В В В В  (a)В В  in the
reference to the Administration of Justice Act 1982, in column 2
replace вЂњ17 to 25вЂќ with вЂњ17 to 22вЂќ;

В В В В В  (b)В В  in the
reference to the Senior Courts Act 1981 вЂ”

В В В В В В  (i)В В  after
paragraph 2 in column 2 insert вЂ”

вЂњ3.В  Sections 106, 107, 110, 112 to
119 (except sections 115(4) and 119(1)), 121 and 128.вЂќ
and

В В В В В  (ii)В В  after
paragraph 2 in column 3 insert вЂ”

вЂњ2A.В  The following provisions of the
Act are modified by вЂ”

(a)В В В В В В В В  in
section 106, replacing вЂњa district probate registrarвЂќ with вЂњthe
judgeвЂќ;

(b)В В В В В В В В  in
section 107 omitting вЂњout of the Principal Registry or any district
probate registryвЂќ and replacing вЂњregistrarвЂќ with
вЂњjudgeвЂќ;

(c)В В В В В В В В  in the
heading to section 110, replacing вЂњCommissioners of the Inland
RevenueвЂќ with вЂњCommissioner of TaxationвЂќ;

(d)В В В В В В В В  in
section 110, replacing вЂ”

(i)В В В В В В В В В 
вЂњthe President of the Family DivisionвЂќ and вЂњPresidentвЂќ with
вЂњjudgeвЂќ;

(ii)В В В В В В В В  вЂњthe
CommissionersвЂќ in each place where it appears with вЂњCommissioner of
TaxationвЂќ; and

(iii)В В В В В В В  вЂњthe
Principal Registry and every district probate registry shallвЂќ with
вЂњthe court mustвЂќ; and

(e)В В В В В В В В  in
section 121(3), replacing вЂњthe Colonial Probates Acts 1892 and
1927вЂќ with вЂњthe Administration of Estates Ordinance
1949вЂќ.

PART 3 -
Miscellaneous amendments

3.В В Amendment of Administration of Estates Ordinance
1949

This
Part amends the Administration of Estates Ordinance
1949.

4.В В Section 2 amended (interpretation)

In
section 2, after the definition of вЂњcourtвЂќ insert
вЂ”

вЂњвЂњgrantвЂќ means a grant of probate or letters of
administration;

вЂњinterested personвЂќ means a person who has an
interest in the estate of the deceased;вЂќ.

PART 1 -
Introductory

Administration of Estates (Amendment) Ordinance 2022

/**/

/**/

2.В В Commencement

This
Ordinance comes into force on publication in the
Gazette.

8.В В Section 7 amended (proof of will in solemn
form)

In
section 7, replace вЂњperson interested in a willвЂќ with вЂњinterested
personвЂќ.

7.В В Section 6 replaced (caveats)

Replace
section 6 with вЂ”

вЂњ6.В В Caveats and citations

(1) В This section applies where вЂ”

В В В В  (a)В В В В  a
caveat is entered before a grant is made; or

В В В В  (b)В В В В  a
citation is issued before or after a grant is made.

(2)В  All interested persons must be summoned to appear
before court to show cause why, in the case of a caveat, the grant
should not issue or, in the case of a citation, the action
specified in the citation should be taken.

(3)В  The court may make such order as is fair and
reasonable in all the circumstances of the case.

(4)В  When any interested person fails to appear, the
court may, after proof of service, proceed as if the proceedings
were ex parte or adjourn on such terms as it may
direct.

(5)В  An interested person aggrieved by an order under
this section may appeal to the Supreme Court within 21
days.вЂќ.

(assented to:В В В В В В В  25
January 2022)

(commencement: upon publication)

(published:В В В В В В В В В В 
31 January 2022)

AN
ORDINANCE

To provide
for a depository for wills of living persons and for
non-contentious probate and administration of estates by the
Supreme Court and connected matters.

ENACTED
by the Legislature of the Falkland Islands вЋЇ

PART 2 -
Amendment of Administration of Estates Ordinance 1949

5.В В New section 2A inserted

After
section 2 insert вЂ”

вЂњ2A.В В Wills depository

(1)В  The Central Registry is a depository for the safe
custody of the wills of living persons and any person may deposit a
will in the Central Registry on payment of the prescribed fee.
[Section 126 1981 c.54]

(2)В  The Wills (Deposit for Safe Custody) Regulations
1978 (SI 1978 No. 1724), with the exception of regulations 3(1) and
6, apply in the Falkland Islands subject to the modifications set
out in Schedule 1.

(3)В  In this section вЂњCentral RegistryвЂќ has the
meaning in section 5 of the Registration Ordinance
1949.вЂќ.

1.В В Title

This
Ordinance is the Administration of Estates (Amendment) Ordinance
2022.

6.В В Section 5 amended (bonds)

In
section 5(2), replace вЂњpersons interestedвЂќ with вЂњinterested
personsвЂќ.

15.В В Section 23 amended (rules of
court)

In
section 23 insert the following subsections, the existing provision
becoming subsection (1) вЂ”

вЂњ(2) В Rules of court may make further or other
provision for the safe custody of the wills of living
persons.

(3)В  The Non-Contentious Probate Rules 1987 (SI No.
1987/2024) apply in the Falkland Islands with the exceptions in
paragraph 1 and subject to the modifications in paragraph 2, of
Schedule 2.вЂќ.

Consequential amendments to Law Revision and Publication
Ordinance 2017

Passed by the Legislature of the Falkland Islands on 20
January 2022.

CHERIE
YVONNE CLIFFORD,

Clerk
of the Legislative Assembly.

10.В В Section 10 amended (service of citations,
etc)

In
section 10, after вЂњCitations,вЂќ insert вЂњcaveats,вЂќ.

19.В В Part 2 of Schedule 1 of Law Revision and
Publication Ordinance amended (secondary legislation)

In Part
2 of Schedule 1, insert the following rows in the appropriate
alphabetic sequence вЂ”

вЂњNon-Contentious Probate Rules
(SI 1987/2024)

1987

Whole
Rules except rule 2(2), rules 3 and 4, rule 5(7), (9) and
(10), rules 5ZA, 5A, 7, 8 and 23, rule 39(2), (6) and (7), rules 40
to 43, rule 47(4) to (7), rule 48(2), rules 49, 50, 57, 60, 62 to
64, 66, and 67 and Schedule 1

The Rules
are subject to the modifications specified in paragraph 2 of the
Schedule to the Administration of Estates Ordinance
1949.

Wills (Deposit for Safe
Custody) Regulations (SI 1978/1724)

1978

Whole
Regulations except regulations 3(1) and 6

The
Regulations are subject to the modifications specified in paragraph
1 of the Schedule to the Administration of Estates Ordinance
1949.вЂќ.

16.В В Schedules 1 and 2 inserted

After
section 23 insert вЂ”

вЂњSCHEDULE 1 - MODIFICATION OF THE WILLS (DEPOSIT FOR
SAFE CUSTODY) REGULATIONS 1978

section
2A

1.В  In subregulation (2) of regulation 2
(interpretation) вЂ”

В В В В  (a)В В В В 
replace the definition of вЂњprescribedвЂќ with
вЂњвЂњprescribedвЂќ means prescribed by rules made under section
23 of the Administration of Estates Ordinance 1949;вЂќ;

В В В В  (b)В В В В  omit
the definition of вЂњthe principal registryвЂќ;

В В В В  (c)В В В В 
replace the definition of вЂњregistrarвЂќ with
вЂњвЂњregistrarвЂќ means the Registrar General appointed for the
purposes of section 4 of the Registration Ordinance
1949;вЂќ;

В В В  (d)В В В В  replace the
definition of вЂњregistryвЂќ with вЂњвЂњregistryвЂќ means the
Central Registry within the meaning of the Registration Ordinance
1949;вЂќ; and

В В В В  (e)В В В В  omit
the definition of вЂњthe Senior RegistrarвЂќ.

2.В  In the Regulations, wherever a term listed in the
left-hand column of the table appears, replace it with the
corresponding term in the right-hand column вЂ”

Term

Substitute
term

principal
registry

registry

172 of the Act

of the Administration of Estates Ordinance 1949

SCHEDULE
2 - MODIFICATION OF THE NON-CONTENTIOUS PROBATE RULES
1987

section
23

1.В  Rule 2(2), rules 3 and 4, rule 5(7), (9) and (10),
rules 5ZA, 5A, 7, 8 and 23, rule 39(2), (6) and (7), rules 40 to
43, rule 47(4) to (7), rule 48(2), rules 49, 50, 57, 60, 62 to 64,
66, and 67 and Schedule 1, are excepted from application to the
Falkland Islands.

2.В  The Rules listed in the left-hand column of the
table are subject to the modifications specified in the right-hand
column вЂ”

Rule

Modification

В 

2 (interpretation)

(1)
В In the definition of вЂњauthorised officerвЂќ replace вЂњthe
PresidentвЂќ with вЂњthe judgeвЂќ.

В 

(2)В 
Replace the definition of вЂ”

В 

вЂњthe
CrownвЂќ with вЂњвЂњthe CrownвЂќ means the Crown in right of the
Falkland Islands;вЂќ and

В 

вЂњregistryвЂќ with вЂњвЂњregistryвЂќ means registry of
the Supreme Court;вЂќ.

(3)В  Omit the definitions
of вЂњdistrict judgeвЂќ, вЂњjudgeвЂќ, вЂњonline portalвЂќ,
вЂњprobate practitionerвЂќ, вЂњregistrarвЂќ, вЂњthe senior
district judgeвЂќ and вЂњthe Treasury SolicitorвЂќ.

(4)В  In the definition of
вЂњpersonal applicantвЂќ omit вЂњor probate
practitionerвЂќ.

(5)В  Insert after
paragraph (1) вЂ”

вЂњ(1A) In these Rules,
references to вЂ”

вЂњdistrict judgeвЂќ,
вЂњjudgeвЂќ вЂњregistrarвЂќ or вЂњthe senior district
judgeвЂќ are to be interpreted as references to a judge of the Supreme Court
appointed under section 88 of the Constitution or an acting judge
appointed under section 89 of the Constitution.вЂќ;
and

вЂњthe Treasury SolicitorвЂќ are to be interpreted as
references to the Attorney General.

В 

5(3)(b) (personal
applications)

Omit вЂњor
probate practitionerвЂќ.

В 

30 (grants where the deceased
died domiciled outside England and Wales)

(1)В 
In the heading to the rule and in paragraph (1) replace вЂњEngland
and WalesвЂќ with вЂњthe Falkland IslandsвЂќ.

В 

(2)В 
In paragraph (3) вЂ”

В 

(a) in sub-paragraph (a)(i) omit вЂњif the will is
in the English or Welsh languageвЂќ; and

В 

(b) in sub-paragraph (b) replace вЂњEngland and
WalesвЂќ with вЂњthe Falkland IslandsвЂќ in both cases where it
appears.

В 

31 (grants to
attorneys)

In
paragraph (3), replace вЂњthe Mental Capacity Act 2005вЂќ with вЂњthe
Assessment and Safeguarding of Adults Ordinance 2020.вЂќ.

В 

32 (grants on behalf of
minors)

Replace
paragraph (1) with вЂ”

В 

вЂњ(1) В Subject to
paragraph (1A), where a person to whom a grant would otherwise be
made is a minor, administration for the minorвЂ™s use and benefit
until the minor attains 18 years of age must, unless otherwise
directed by the court, be granted to a person who has or is deemed
to have parental responsibility for the minor.

В 

(1A) Where the minor is sole executor and has no
interest in the residuary estate of the deceased, administration
for the use and benefit of the minor must be granted to the person
entitled to the residuary estate, unless otherwise directed by the
court.вЂќ.

В 

35 (grants in case of lack of
mental capacity)

(1)В 
In paragraph (1), omit вЂњwithin the meaning of the Mental Capacity
Act 2005 referred to in paragraph (2) belowвЂќ.

В 

(2)В 
Omit вЂњwithin the meaning of the Mental Capacity Act 2005вЂќ in
вЂ”

(a) the introductory words and in sub-paragraph
(b) of paragraph (2); and

В 

(b) paragraph (4).

В 

(3)
В After paragraph (5) insert вЂ”

В 

вЂњ(6)В  In this rule, the expression вЂњperson
who lacks capacityвЂќ has the meaning in section 4 of the
Assessment and Safeguarding of Adults Ordinance 2020.вЂќ.

В 

39 (resealing under Colonial
Probates Acts 1892 and 1927)

(1)В 
Replace the heading with вЂњResealing under the Administration of
Estates Ordinance 1949вЂќ.

В 

(2)В 
In paragraph 1, replace вЂњthe Colonial Probates Acts 1892 and 1927вЂќ
with вЂњthe Administration of Estates Ordinance 1949вЂќ and вЂњthose
ActsвЂќ with вЂњthat OrdinanceвЂќ.

В 

(3)В 
In paragraph 5, replace вЂњsubsection (1) of section 2 of the
Colonial Probates Acts 1892вЂќ with вЂњsection 22(1) of the
Administration of Estates Ordinance 1949вЂќ.

В 

44 (caveats)

(1)В 
In paragraph (1) omit вЂњ; provided that вЂ¦ caveat is
enteredвЂќ.

В 

(2)В 
Replace paragraph (2) with вЂ”

В 

вЂњ(2) В Any person wishing to enter a caveat
(вЂњthe caveatorвЂќ) may do so by giving notice in writing to
the court.вЂќ.

В 

(3)В 
Omit paragraphs (3) to (10), (12), (13) and (15).

В 

(4)В 
Replace paragraph (11) with вЂ”

В 

вЂњ(11)
В A caveator may withdraw the caveat at any time by giving
notice to the court and any interested person.вЂќ.

В 

(5)В 
In paragraph (14), replace вЂњunder paragraphs (7) or (12) of this
rule or under rule 45(4) or rule 46(3)вЂќ with вЂњeither by order of the judge or on
withdrawalвЂќ.

В 

45 (probate actions)

In
paragraph (1), omit вЂњbeing advised by the court concerned of
theвЂќ.

В 

46 (citations)

Replace
rule 46 with вЂ”

В 

вЂњ46(1) В Any person who has an
interest in the estate of the deceased (вЂњthe citorвЂќ) may
apply to the court to issue a citation against one or more
executors of the estate (вЂњthe citeeвЂќ).

В 

(2)В 
An application to issue a citation must include вЂ”

В 

(a) a statement of the facts and matters relied on
in support of the application;

В 

(b) the names and contact details of the citees
and any other interested persons known to the citor; and

В 

(c) the action that the citor considers should be
taken to ensure the effective administration of the deceasedвЂ™s
estate,

В 

and be
supported by a statement of truth.

В 

(3)В 
The citor must serve the citation on the citees unless the court
otherwise directs and all other interested persons must be notified
of the citation.

В 

(4)
В A citation may be withdrawn with the permission of the
court.вЂќ.

В 

65 (appeals from district
judges or registrars)

(1)В 
In paragraph (1) replace вЂњor registrar shall be made вЂ¦ to a judgeвЂќ
with вЂњlies to the Court of
AppealвЂќ.

В 

(2)В 
Omit paragraphs (2) and (3).

В 

68 (application to pending
proceedings)

Replace
вЂњcome into forceвЂќ with вЂњare applied in the
Falkland IslandsвЂќ.

В 

13.В В Section 12 amended (witnesses)

In
section 12(1) after вЂњany person whom it thinks fit to examine,вЂќ
insert вЂњwhether or not any legal proceedings are
pending,вЂќ.

20.В В Administration of Estates Rules 1949
amended

In the
Administration of Estates Rules 1949 вЂ”

В В В В В  (a)В В  revoke rule
2 and Schedule 1; and

В В В В В  (b)В В  in Schedule
2 вЂ”

В В В В В В  (i)В В 
replace items 2 and 3 with вЂ”

вЂњ2.В  For the entry of a caveat or
issue of a citation under section 6 of the Administration of
Estates Ordinance
1949В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В 
В В В В В В В В В В В 
В В В  ВЈ21

3.В  For inspection of records under
section 11A of the Administration of Estates Ordinance 1949
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В 
ВЈ10вЂќ;

В В В В В  (ii)В В  replace
item 6 with вЂ”

вЂњ6.В  For depositing a will in the
Central Registry under section 2A of the Administration of Estates
Ordinance
1949В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В 
В В В В В В В  ВЈ10.50вЂќ.

12.В В New section 11A inserted

After
section 11 insert вЂ”

вЂњ11A.В В Inspection of records

(1)В  All wills and other documents relating to the estate of a deceased person are
under the control of the court from the date on which the first
application is made to the court relating to that estate, whether
to enter a caveat, issue a citation or for a grant of probate or
letters of administration.

(2)В  The Registrar General must deliver up any
documents deposited under section 2A to which subsection (1)
applies immediately on receiving a request from the Registrar of
the Supreme Court.

(3)В  Subject to subsection (1) and any rules made
under, or applying by virtue of, section 23, any person may inspect
such documents relating to the estate of a deceased person at the
court by appointment during normal business hours on payment of
such fee as may be prescribed.

(4)В  Subject to the approval of the court, the
Registrar of the Supreme Court may provide a certified copy of any
will or part of will or of a grant to any person on payment of the
prescribed fee.вЂќ.

21.В В Registration Regulations 1949
amended

In
Schedule 2 to the Registration Regulations 1949, omit item
6.

9.В В Section 8 amended (absentee personal
representative)

In
section 8 вЂ”

В В В В В  (a)В В  in
subsection (1), replace вЂњAny person interested in the estate of a
deceased personвЂќ with вЂњAny interested personвЂќ; and

В В В В В  (b)В В  in
subsection (2) replace вЂњsection 6(1)вЂќ with вЂњsection
6(2)вЂќ.

14.В В New section 12A inserted

After
section 12 insert вЂ”

вЂњ12A.В В Orders for inventory and
account

(1)В  The court may on its own motion or on application
by an interested person, order the executor or administrator of an
estate (вЂњthe granteeвЂќ) to provide an inventory of the assets
that were part of, or have been brought into, the estate and to
account for payments or transfers out of the estate of any monies
or other property.

(2)В  The court may, if not satisfied that a grantee is
administering an estate efficiently or expeditiously, by order make
a grant in respect of the estate to another interested person or
the Official Administrator either in addition to, or instead of,
the grantee and in the latter case must revoke the original
grant.

(3)В  A grantee may appeal to the Supreme Court against
an order under this section within 21 days of the date of the order
in question.вЂќ.

This
printed impression has been carefully compared by me with the Bill
which has passed the Legislative Assembly and is found by me to be
a true and correctly printed copy of the said Bill.

CHERIE
YVONNE CLIFFORD,

Clerk
of the Legislative Assembly.

Amendments to Regulations

11.В В Section 11 amended (records)

In
section 11 replace вЂњRegistrarвЂќ with вЂњRegistrar of the Supreme
CourtвЂќ.
